title: T-008 — scheduler_config table (PLE-005)
prompt:
# T-008 — scheduler_config table (PLE-005)

PRD: docs/prd/07-PLANNING-EXT-PRD.md §17.1 PLE-005, §17.3 backlog item 1
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Introduce `scheduler_config` table holding per-org default run parameters and alert thresholds (PLE-005 Cards 1-2 persistence target).

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/0077_scheduler_config.sql`: CREATE TABLE scheduler_config per PLE-005 DDL stub (`org_id uuid PK`, default_horizon_days, default_include_forecast, solver_timeout_seconds, alerts JSONB, updated_by, updated_at) + ENABLE+FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY.
2. CHECK default_horizon_days IN (7, 14) and solver_timeout_seconds BETWEEN 10 AND 300.
3. Policy `scheduler_config_org_context` USING (`org_id = app.current_org_id()`) WITH CHECK (`org_id = app.current_org_id()`) — the foundation function from `packages/db/migrations/002-rls-baseline.sql`; never `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` GUC.
4. Drizzle schema entry.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0077_scheduler_config.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/scheduler_config.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/__tests__/scheduler-config.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration 0077 applied, when \d scheduler_config runs, then all PLE-005 columns + horizon CHECK + timeout CHECK + RLS+FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + org_id PK exist
2. Given an INSERT with default_horizon_days=21, when executed, then it fails CHECK
3. Given two orgs and app.set_org_context called per org, when each SELECTs as app_user, then each only sees its own config row
4. Given pg_policies is inspected, when the scheduler_config policy is listed, then qual/withcheck reference app.current_org_id() and contain no current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id') GUC

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/db/__tests__/scheduler-config.test.ts asserting CHECK + RLS (via app.set_org_context as app_user) + org_id PK
- Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test scheduler-config'

## Risk red lines
- Do not read `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` directly — use the foundation `app.current_org_id()` function so the NULL-safe setter contract is preserved.
- Do not use `tenant_id` as the business-scope column; per Wave0 v4.3 lock it is `org_id`.
- Do not skip CHECK constraints (UI relies on these for validation rendering)
- Do not skip RLS/FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY
- Do not forget to make org_id the PRIMARY KEY (one row per org)

pipeline_inputs.description: Create scheduler_config table per PLE-005 with horizon and timeout CHECK constraints + foundation-app.current_org_id() RLS.
pipeline_inputs.details: Migration 0077 emits CREATE TABLE scheduler_config per PLE-005 DDL stub with CHECK constraints. org_id is PK (one row per org). ENABLE+FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY with policy `scheduler_config_org_context` USING/WITH CHECK `org_id = app.current_org_id()`. Drizzle schema mirrors columns. RED test verifies CHECK rejection + RLS isolation via app_user + app.set_org_context. RLS uses foundation `app.current_org_id()` function (Wave0 decision; see `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-architecture-and-cross-cutting-gaps.md` §Tenant-context).

Do not implement audit log here (covered by app-layer)
